to kings , and freedom to the world
that over their heights unfurled , Bear death to kings , and freedom to the world
The purest frenzy of poetic fire

the smooth , the emblematic song Flow like thy genial juices over my tongue , Could those mild morsels in my numbers chime , And , as they roll in substance , roll in rhyme , No more thy awkward unpoetic name Should shun the Muse , or prejudice thy fame

, the French of course Polante ; Even in thy native regions , how I blush To hear the Pennsylvanians call thee Much
thy just defence With logic clear , they thus explained the sense : — " In haste the boiling cauldron , over the blaze , " Receives and cooks the ready-powdered maize

the boiling cauldron , over the blaze
Thy name is Hasty-Pudding ! thus our sires Were wont to greet thee fuming from their fires
to share thy bounties with the beast
my natal morn , And all my bones were made of Indian corn
my natal morn , And all my bones
Let beans and corn their sweetest juices blend
drench them in its yellow tide
Some talk of Hoe-cake , fair Virginia's pride , Rich Johnny-cake this mouth

the lessons that the Muse shall bring
But since , OH man ! thy life and health
the race that courts her yielding soil
the green spire declares the sprouting root
your nursling from each greedy foe , The insidious worm , the all-devouring crow

the standards high , And shoots the tall top-gallants to the sky

each red ear a general kiss he gains , With each smut ear she smuts the luckless swains

The headlong stream that over the mill-dam breaks )
with clean salt she seasons well the food , Then strews the flour
A great resource in those bleak wintery days
How oft thy teats these pious hands
To this in future I confine my Muse

not well in those substantial things , Whose mass adhesive to the metal clings

so much above the lore of art
